add_menu.correct_defaults:
Concrete edits to def menu(...) in src/m4l_builder/ui.py (~line 329), mirroring def tab (lines 179-237):

1) FIX THE SHORTNAME BUG — pass shortname into the spec:
   spec = _resolve_parameter_spec(varname, parameter=parameter, shortname=shortname, parameter_type=2, minimum=0, maximum=enum_max, initial=0, initial_enable=True, enum=options)
   (today shortname is a param but never reaches the spec, so the short display name is lost.)

2) ADD THEMED COLOR ARGS with active* mirroring. Add to the signature:
   bgcolor: list = None, bgoncolor: list = None, textcolor: list = None, textoncolor: list = None
   Then after building box, before box.update(kwargs):
     if bgcolor:    box["bgcolor"] = bgcolor;       box["activebgcolor"] = bgcolor
     if bgoncolor:  box["bgoncolor"] = bgoncolor;   box["activebgoncolor"] = bgoncolor
     if textcolor:  box["textcolor"] = textcolor;   box["activetextcolor"] = textcolor
     if textoncolor:box["textoncolor"]=textoncolor; box["activetextoncolor"]=textoncolor
   Rationale (verbatim from the live.tab doc and our doc reference): device is active=1, so the colors actually shown are the active* variants; the bare ones only render when bypassed. A single color arg must theme BOTH states. Explicit active* via kwargs still wins because box.update(kwargs) runs last.

3) DO NOT add unitstyle — live.menu is an Enum param and has no unit (leave numeric unitstyle to dial/numbox/slider).

4) Leave parameter setup as-is — it is already correct (parameter_type=2, min 0, max len(options)-1, initial 0/enabled, enum=options, parameter_enable=1, saved_attribute_attributes).

GOTCHAS:
- options must be non-empty; enum_max = max(len(options)-1, 0) already guards the empty case (avoid maximum < 0).
- The Enum labels ARE the option strings; the parameter's stored/automated value is the index (float on outlet 3). When you read automation you get the index, not the label.
- Because parameter_type=2, do NOT also set a numeric unitstyle or a wide int range — Enum stays 0..N-1.
- Keep numoutlets=3 / outlettype ["","","float"]; the param value is on the 3rd outlet, the 1st outlet is the raw item output.
add_menu.doc_section:
## ✅ live.menu  (dropdown enum selector)

`live.menu` = a dropdown backed by a real, automatable Live parameter. The visible
items ARE the enum labels; the stored/automated value is the **index** (0..N-1, float on
the 3rd outlet). `add_menu(options=[...])` registers it as a `parameter_type=2` (Enum)
param with `minimum=0`, `maximum=len(options)-1`, `initial=0`.

- **Colors (the active\* rule):** menu body = `activebgcolor`; highlighted/selected item =
  `activebgoncolor`; label + arrow = `activetextcolor` (on-text `activetextoncolor`). The
  bare `bgcolor`/`bgoncolor`/`textcolor`/`textoncolor` render **only when the device is
  bypassed** (active=0), so the kit mirrors each color arg to its `active*` twin — one arg
  themes both states. Explicit `active*` via kwargs still wins.
- **No `unitstyle`** — Enum params have no unit (unitstyle is for numeric dial/numbox/slider).
- **`shortname`** sets the short visual/automation name (≈5-7 chars). `bordercolor` /
  `focusbordercolor` for the box edge; `fontsize`/`fontname` for the label.
- **Prefer over `live.tab`** when there are ≥5 options, long word labels, or little
  horizontal room (one compact box regardless of count). Use `live.tab` for ≤4 short modes
  with room to show them all. Use **`umenu` only** for non-parameter, transient lists
  (no automation, not saved with the preset) — `live.menu` is the automatable choice.
- **Sizing:** ~15-18 px tall (dense native row); width ≈ longest-label·7-8 px + ~16 px for
  the arrow. Label the control with a `live.comment` above it.

> Note: the synced Max docs have no dedicated `live.menu` page; its color/parameter model
> is identical to `live.tab` (see `live-tab-44c2c0dd94d9.html`), which documents the
> active\*/bare split verbatim.
add_menu.when_to_use:
vs a custom v8ui control: ALWAYS prefer native live.menu for an enum/mode/preset selector. It is a first-class automatable Live parameter, themable, and free. Reserve custom v8ui only for heroes — drag-curve editors, XY pads, bespoke handle/track looks — never for a plain list pick.

vs sibling native widgets (enum-param family):
- live.menu (dropdown): use when there are MANY options (≈5+), or when horizontal space is tight, or the choices are long words (filter type, oscillator shape, routing target, sync rate, preset slot). One compact box regardless of option count.
- live.tab (segmented): prefer when there are FEW options (≤4) AND there is horizontal room — all choices visible at once, one click, no dropdown. Better for 2-4 short modes.
- live.toggle: exactly 2 states / on-off — cheaper than a 2-item menu.
- live.text / live.button: a single labeled momentary or mode button, not a list.
Rule of thumb: ≤4 short options with room → live.tab; otherwise → live.menu.
vs umenu (non-param): umenu has NO parameter storage (not automatable, not saved with the device, not mappable). Use umenu only for transient UI lists that must NOT become a Live parameter (e.g. a file/patch picker driving patcher logic). For anything the user should automate or that must persist with the preset, use live.menu.
add_menu.sizing:
Compact premium device guidance (presentation_rect [x,y,w,h], px):
- Height: 15-18 px (match live.numbox/live.tab row height; ~17 px is the dense native row). Don't exceed ~20.
- Width: size to the LONGEST label plus the dropdown arrow — roughly 7-8 px per character + ~16 px for the arrow/padding. Short enums (e.g. "LP/HP/BP"): 56-72 px. Word labels (e.g. "Lowpass/Highpass/Notch"): 90-120 px.
- Keep fontsize modest (~10-11) so it reads at the dense row height; set fontname to the device's UI font for consistency.
- Align left/top with neighboring live.numbox / live.tab on the same row so the grid stays tight. Put a live.comment label above (not inside) for the control name, per the native-first dense layout.

add_umenu.correct_defaults:
Concrete changes to def umenu() in /Users/squidbot/Projects/m4l-builder/src/m4l_builder/ui.py (~line 1009). The structural box is already correct (maxclass, numinlets 1, numoutlets 3, outlettype ["int","",""], presentation wiring, optional items). ADD theming + guardrails:
1. Items normalization: accept a Python list and comma-join into the umenu `items` string (real umenu stores items as a comma-joined symbol, not a JSON array). Keep accepting a pre-joined string. Empty entries -> separators.
2. Theming kwargs (classic Max, NO active* mirroring): add optional bgcolor, bgfillcolor (dict), textcolor, textcolor2, fontname="Ableton Sans Medium", fontsize=10.0. Set each on the box only when provided (mirror the comment()/panel() pattern). Do NOT add any active*/bgoncolor logic — that rule is live.*-only and umenu has no such attrs. Default to inheriting the patcher/theme so an untouched umenu still reads as part of the device.
3. parameter_enable: set explicitly to 0 in the box so it is unambiguous that this is the non-parameter dropdown (prevents confusion with add_menu). Do NOT add a ParameterSpec/saved_attribute_attributes path — that is add_menu's job.
4. Optional dynamic-menu passthrough: expose prefix/autopopulate/depth via **kwargs (already works through box.update(kwargs)); document them.
5. Keep box.update(kwargs) LAST so callers can override anything (including dropping in raw color dicts).
6. Docstring + a one-line guard note: "Non-parameter dropdown — no automation/preset/undo. For an automatable enum use add_menu (live.menu)."
GOTCHAS to encode: (a) umenu does not persist its selection as a device parameter — if you wire it to control DSP you must save state yourself (pattrstorage or a backing param). (b) bgfillcolor overrides bgcolor when both set. (c) textcolor2 (highlight) is separate from textcolor — set both for a coherent theme. (d) for prefix/autopopulate menus the saved items list is empty by design; do not pass items.
add_umenu.doc_section:
## ✅ add_umenu  (umenu — classic dropdown, NON-parameter)

- **Classic Max object, NOT a `live.*` widget.** It stores **no parameter**: no
  automation, no preset recall, no undo, no Live MIDI-map. The **active\* color rule
  does NOT apply** — umenu has no `active*` attributes at all.
- **Outlets (fixed):** `["int","",""]` → outlet 0 = selected **index** (int), outlet 1 =
  item **text/symbol**, outlet 2 = dump/right. `numinlets 1`, `numoutlets 3`.
- **`items`**: comma-joined string (kit accepts a list and joins). An **empty entry =
  separator** line. Dynamic menus: leave `items` empty and use `prefix` (folder/collective)
  + `autopopulate 1` + `depth` — these fill at load, so the saved list is empty by design.
- **Color attrs (classic, not active\*):** `bgcolor` (flat) · `bgfillcolor` (gradient
  **dict** `{"type":"color","color":[…],"color1":[…],"color2":[…],"angle":270.0,
  "proportion":0.39,"autogradient":0}` — same shape as `add_panel`; **overrides `bgcolor`**)
  · `textcolor` (item) · **`textcolor2`** (the *highlighted/hover* item — easy to forget) ·
  `bordercolor`/`elementcolor` · `fontname` / `fontsize`.
- **When to use vs `add_menu`:** `add_menu` (**live.menu**) registers a **type-2 enum
  parameter** → automates, saves, undoes, maps. That is the default for any device-setting
  dropdown. Use **`add_umenu` only for non-parameter / runtime-populated lists**: file &
  preset browsers, MIDI/audio port pickers, dev selectors, or menus wired through patch
  logic. vs **live.tab**: prefer a tab for a small fixed enum (≤4–5) with horizontal room;
  reach for a dropdown when options are many, variable, or runtime-populated.
- **Sizing:** h **16** px (corpus uses 20; 16 is tighter/premium and matches `live.menu`
  rows), w **70–120** for short labels / **120–180** for long or file lists; `fontsize`
  10–11. Align baselines with sibling `live.numbox`/`live.menu` on the same y-grid.
- **Gotcha:** because it is not a parameter, a umenu controlling DSP needs you to persist
  state yourself (a backing param or `pattrstorage`); the selection will not survive a
  preset/undo on its own.
add_umenu.when_to_use:
vs custom v8ui: never build a custom dropdown in v8ui — a popup menu is exactly what native umenu/live.menu do well, and a custom one loses OS-native list rendering, scrolling, and keyboard nav. Reserve v8ui for heroes/drag-curves/XY pads, not list pickers.
vs add_menu (live.menu) — THE decision that matters: live.menu is a live.* control that registers a type-2 (enum) PARAMETER → it automates, saves with the preset, supports undo, and is Live-mappable. umenu does NONE of that. So:
  - Use add_menu (live.menu) for any user-facing CHOICE that is a device setting and should persist/automate (filter type, LFO shape, routing mode, oversampling). This is the default for ~95% of dropdowns in a finished device.
  - Use add_umenu ONLY for NON-parameter, often runtime-populated lists: file/preset/sample browsers (prefix + autopopulate + depth), MIDI/audio port pickers, dev or debug selectors, or a menu whose contents are pushed in via messages and whose result is wired through patch logic (not stored as a param).
vs live.tab: live.tab is the premium pick for a small fixed enum (<=4-5 options) when there is horizontal room — it shows all options at once. Reach for a dropdown (live.menu, or umenu) only when options are many (>5), variable-length, or runtime-populated, or when vertical space is tight.
vs radiogroup: radiogroup for a short vertical exclusive set where you want every option visible; dropdown when collapsed space is needed.
add_umenu.sizing:
Compact native dropdown, in presentation_rect [x, y, w, h]:
- Height: 15-18 px (corpus real-world umenus use h=20; for a dense premium device 16 reads tighter, matching live.menu/live.numbox rows). Keep all dropdowns in a row on the same h.
- Width: 70-120 px for short fixed labels; 120-180 px when entries are long or runtime-populated (file names). Real corpus example used w=81 and w=150.
- fontsize 10-11 to match the kit's live.comment/live.menu.
- Align dropdown baselines with sibling live.numbox/live.menu rows (same h, same y-grid) so a config strip looks uniform.
- For a single full-width config selector at the top of a device, w can span the panel minus margins (e.g. ~180-220) at h=16.

add_button.correct_defaults:
Concrete changes to `def button(...)` in src/m4l_builder/ui.py:457 (mirror the tab()/toggle() pattern):

1. ACTIVE* COLOR MIRRORING (the headline fix). Add keyword args and mirror bare->active so a single color arg themes the state you actually see:
   - add params: `bgcolor: list = None, bgoncolor: list = None, activebgcolor: list = None, activebgoncolor: list = None, bordercolor: list = None`
   - logic:
       on = activebgoncolor or bgoncolor    # the flash color
       off = activebgcolor or bgcolor        # the resting color
       if off:  box["activebgcolor"] = off  ; box.setdefault("bgcolor", off)
       if on:   box["activebgoncolor"] = on ; box.setdefault("bgoncolor", on)
       if bordercolor: box["bordercolor"] = bordercolor
     (Always set the active* twin; optionally also set the bare one so the bypassed look matches. Explicit active* kwargs win over the bare alias — same precedence tab() uses.)
   - sensible themed default: if no color passed, set activebgoncolor to the device ACCENT and activebgcolor to the panel/surface color so the trigger reads as accent-on-dark instead of grey-on-grey.

2. TRANSITION. Add `transition: int = 0` kwarg; only emit when non-zero: `if transition: box["transition"] = transition`. 0 = bang on press (correct momentary default). Pass 2 for press-and-release.

3. BLINKTIME. Add `blinktime: int = None`; emit when set (Max default 100ms). Useful to lengthen the flash on a hero trigger.

4. PARAMETER VISIBILITY (the gotcha). For a pure momentary trigger you usually do NOT want it in automation lanes. Surface this via the existing ParameterSpec `invisible`/visibility path: support `stored: bool = True`; when False set the spec to the 'Visible (Not Stored)' mode so the button is Push-mappable but not recorded. Default stays stored=True to preserve current behavior.

5. unitstyle: leave alone — live.button has no numeric readout. Do NOT inherit dial percent. The factory's parameter_type=2 + enum=[off,on] is correct; keep it.

GOTCHA / no-change: bordercolor/focusbordercolor only render in Live's MIDI-mapping mode per the docs, so don't rely on bordercolor for the normal look — color via activebgcolor/activebgoncolor. Keep parameter_enable=1 and saved_attribute_attributes (removing them de-parameterizes the button). numoutlets stays 1, outlettype [""] (bang).
add_button.doc_section:
## ✅ live.button  (momentary trigger / bang)

`live.button` = momentary trigger. On any input it **flashes** and sends a **bang**; it is
a real automatable Live parameter storing **0/1** (the bang fires on the transition you pick).
Use it for one-shot actions (retrig, randomize, panic, tap, reset) — for a *latching* on/off
use `live.toggle`.

- **Color (active* rule!):** the device runs `active=1`, so the colors you SEE are
  **`activebgoncolor`** (the **flash / ON** bg) and **`activebgcolor`** (the **resting / OFF**
  bg). Bare `bgcolor`/`bgoncolor` only show when **bypassed**. `add_button` mirrors a passed
  `bgcolor`→`activebgcolor` and `bgoncolor`→`activebgoncolor` so one arg themes the live state;
  pass `activebgoncolor=ACCENT` for an accent flash. Unthemed default = grey-on-grey.
- **`transition` [int] 0** — WHICH value change emits the bang (this is the behavior knob;
  `live.button` has **no `outputmode`**): `0 = Zero→One` (bang on **press**, default),
  `1 = One→Zero` (bang on **release**), `2 = Both` (press + release → momentary *gate*).
- **`blinktime` [int] 100** — flash duration (ms). Bump to ~150 for a big hero trigger.
- **`bordercolor` / `focusbordercolor`** — per the Max docs these render **only in Live's
  MIDI-mapping mode**, so don't rely on them for the normal look; color via the bg attrs.
- **Parameter:** Type = Enum, Range/Enum = `off on` (the factory sets `parameter_type=2` +
  `enum=["off","on"]`, `parameter_enable=1`). `unitstyle` is irrelevant (no numeric readout) —
  never let it inherit the dial's percent.
- **Gotcha — automation pollution:** a momentary control usually shouldn't sit in automation
  lanes. Set **Parameter Visibility = 'Visible (Not Stored)'** (`add_button(..., stored=False)`)
  to keep it Push-mappable but out of automation/presets. Default stays stored.
- **Sizing:** 16–20px square inline; ~22–28px with a `live.comment` label; up to ~40×28 for a
  hero action. No internal text — pair with a comment for the label.
- **When to use:** native `live.button` is the premium default for any "fire once" action
  (mappable, themeable). Reserve **custom v8ui** for drawn glyphs (play/record), press-and-hold,
  or bespoke hero shading; use **`live.text`** when the trigger needs a text label; use
  **`live.toggle`** when the state must latch.
add_button.when_to_use:
vs a custom v8ui control: Use native live.button for any momentary "do it now" action — trigger envelope/LFO retrig, randomize, panic/clear, freeze, tap, reset-to-default, fire a sequence. It is parameter-enabled, Push-mappable, and theme-able with two colors, so it is the premium default. Reserve a custom v8ui button ONLY when you need a drawn glyph/icon (play/record symbol), a non-rectangular hit shape, a press-and-hold gesture with custom feedback, or a hero CTA with bespoke shading the active* colors can't express. For a labeled trigger, prefer live.text in button appearance over a custom control.

vs sibling native widgets:
- live.toggle / this button both store 0/1, but TOGGLE LATCHES (stays on) and BUTTON is MOMENTARY (flashes, returns to 0). Use toggle for on/off state (bypass, enable); use button for a one-shot event/bang.
- live.text (button appearance) when you need a TEXT LABEL on the trigger ("RESET", "TAP"); live.button is the compact unlabeled square/pill — pair it with a live.comment label for the densest look.
- live.tab when the action is "pick one of N" (segmented) rather than "fire once".
- Note transition=2 (Both) makes live.button behave gate-like (bang on press AND release) without latching — use that instead of a toggle when you want momentary gate semantics.
add_button.sizing:
Native live.button is square-ish and dense. For a compact premium device:
- Rail / inline trigger: 16x16 to 20x20 px (matches a row of toggles/numboxes).
- Standard labeled trigger (with a live.comment caption beside/under it): ~22x18 to 28x22 px.
- Hero / primary action (e.g. big RANDOMIZE): up to ~40x28 px, optionally raise `blinktime` to ~150ms so the flash is legible at that size.
- Keep height aligned to the row's other native controls (toggles/numboxes ~15–18px) for a clean grid. The button has no internal text, so width is purely aesthetic — don't oversize; let an adjacent live.comment carry the label.

add_textbutton.correct_defaults:
Concrete changes to def textbutton(...) in src/m4l_builder/ui.py (~line 970):

1. DO NOT add active* mirroring. Unlike tab()/toggle(), textbutton has no active* attrs — the bare bgcolor/bgoncolor/textcolor/textoncolor already are the live colors. Keep the existing bare-color pass-through; just give them themed defaults instead of None so the control isn't legacy-grey. Suggested premium-dark defaults (override via kwargs): bgcolor ≈ panel fill [0.16 0.16 0.18 1.], bgoncolor ≈ accent [your RUPTURE accent], textcolor ≈ [0.78 0.78 0.82 1.], textoncolor ≈ [1. 1. 1. 1.].

2. Make mode accept names, mirroring how button() takes mode:str. Map "bang"/"button"/"momentary"→0, "toggle"/"switch"→1. Current signature already has mode:int=0 — accept str|int and normalize. Default stays 0 (momentary bang).

3. usebgoncolor gotcha — when mode resolves to toggle (1), auto-set box["usebgoncolor"]=1 so the on-background actually paints (otherwise a toggle looks identical on/off). Also expose usebgoncolor as an explicit kwarg (default None) so button-mode users can opt in to a pressed flash. This is the single most important fix.

4. Expose outputmode (default leave unset → Max default 1/Mouse-up; or set 0 for snappier mousedown triggers). Document that default fire-on-release differs from live.toggle.

5. texton/textoff handling — the factory currently exposes texton/textoff but only sets them if not None. Keep, but document they ONLY matter in toggle mode. In button mode set just text. (Minor: the Max attr for the off label is `text`, and `texton` for on; the factory's `textoff` kwarg should map to `text` — verify it's not writing a non-existent `textoff` attr. Max has NO `textoff` attribute; the off label IS `text`. The current code writing box["textoff"] is a BUG — that attribute is ignored. Map textoff→text, or drop textoff and use the positional `text` arg.)

6. rounded — default 0; allow a small value (e.g. 3.) to match themed siblings; pass through as-is.

7. No unitstyle, no needlemode, no parameter spec / saved_attribute_attributes, no parameter_enable block needed for the common case — textbutton is a fire-and-forget trigger with no Live automation storage. Do NOT add a ParameterSpec like tab()/dial do. If the caller truly needs an automatable labeled button, steer them to live_text() instead (see when_to_use).

8. fontsize default — set a compact default (e.g. 10.) so dense rails read right; pass spacing_x/spacing_y through (default 4.) but allow smaller (2.) for very compact buttons.

LOAD/GOTCHA SUMMARY: (a) usebgoncolor:1 required for visible on-state; (b) `textoff` is not a real attr — use `text`; (c) outputmode defaults to Mouse-up; (d) outlet 2 is hover (int), not a second trigger; (e) it is NOT automatable in Live — no parameter is stored.
add_textbutton.doc_section:
## ✅ textbutton  (labeled trigger / labeled toggle — plain Max U/I, NOT live.*)

`textbutton` is a regular **Max** object (`maxclass "textbutton"`, category U/I), **not** a
`live.*` control. So the **active\* color rule does NOT apply** — `textbutton` has **no**
`activebgcolor`/`activebgoncolor`/etc. The **bare** `bgcolor` / `bgoncolor` / `textcolor` /
`textoncolor` ARE the colors you see in a running device. (Do **not** mirror to `active*` here
the way `tab()`/`toggle()` do — those attrs are ignored.)

- **`mode` [int] : 0** — `0` = **Button** (momentary: bangs on click, stores nothing),
  `1` = **Toggle** (latches on/off, swaps `text`↔`texton`, outputs 0/1). Factory accepts
  `"bang"`/`"toggle"`.
- **`usebgoncolor` [int] : 0** — **GOTCHA:** with `0`, `bgoncolor` is never painted, so a
  toggle looks identical on and off. **Set `1`** to show the on/pressed background. Factory
  auto-sets `1` in toggle mode.
- **`outputmode` [int] : 1** — `0` = Mouse **down**, `1` = Mouse **up** (default fires on
  release; opposite of `live.toggle`'s mousedown habit).
- **`text` [symbol] : "Button"** — the **off/idle** label. **`texton` [symbol] : "Button On"**
  — the on label (toggle mode only). ⚠ there is **no `textoff` attribute** — the off label *is*
  `text` (writing `textoff` is silently ignored).
- **Colors [4 floats RGBA]:** `bgcolor` (off bg), `bgoncolor` (on bg, needs `usebgoncolor 1`),
  `textcolor` (off text), `textoncolor` (on text); optional hover `textovercolor` +
  `usetextovercolor 1`. Max legacy defaults are ugly grey (`bgcolor 0.75…`, `bgoncolor 0.55…`)
  — set themed colors or it renders flat-grey. **Obsolete / do not set:** `bordercolor`,
  `borderoncolor`, `bgovercolor`.
- **`rounded` [float] : 0.** corner radius · **`spacing_x`/`spacing_y` [float] : 4.** text inset
  · **`truncate` [int] : 1** (right-clips overflow silently — size width generously) ·
  **`blinktime` [int] : 150** ms flash · `fontsize`/`fontname` common-box.
- **Outlets (3):** `0` = **bang on mouse-up** (the trigger) · `1` = item symbol (label) ·
  `2` = **idle/hover** int (1/0 as mouse enters/leaves), *not* a second click.
- **No Live parameter:** `textbutton` is **not** stored/automatable (no `unitstyle`, no
  parameter type, no `saved_attribute_attributes`). `param_connect` can bind it to a `gen~`
  param and `parameter_mappable` allows MIDI map, but for an **automatable** labeled button use
  **`live_text()`** instead.

**Use when:** a momentary command that stores nothing — `Reset`, `Randomize`, `Panic`,
`Apply`. **Prefer `live_text()`** when the on/off state must be **saved/automated** (it's the
live.* sibling with `active*` theming + a stored param). **`live.button`** = tiny round bang,
no label; **`live.toggle`** = on/off, no label; **`ubutton`** = invisible click-zone over art.
**Sizing:** ~52–64×18 px standard, ~34–44×16 px inline, ~80–110×20–22 px hero; min width
≈ `0.55·fontsize·max(len(text),len(texton)) + 2·spacing_x`, min height ≈ `fontsize+10`.
Reserve **v8ui** only for animated/icon hero transport buttons native can't draw.
add_textbutton.when_to_use:
vs CUSTOM v8ui: Always prefer textbutton (or live.text) for a plain labeled trigger/toggle — a button is exactly the kind of flat, native control the NATIVE-FIRST rule says to keep native. Reserve v8ui only for hero transport buttons that need custom iconography, multi-state art, or animated press feedback that textbutton's flat fill+label can't draw.

vs SIBLING NATIVE WIDGETS:
- vs live.text — live.text is the live.* sibling and IS an automatable, MIDI-mappable Live PARAMETER that stores 0/1 with full active* theming (activebgoncolor/activetextoncolor) and appearance modes (Default/Label/LCD). Use live.text (the builder's live_text()) when the button's on/off state must be saved with the device, automated, or theme-correct under bypass. Use textbutton when you just need a momentary command bang (e.g. "Reset", "Randomize", "Apply", "Panic") that fires an action and stores nothing.
- vs live.button — a tiny round momentary bang with no text label; use when you want an icon-less dot trigger and the label lives in an adjacent comment. textbutton when the label must be ON the button.
- vs live.toggle — a pure on/off square with no text; use for compact bypass/enable switches. textbutton (toggle mode) when the toggle needs a word on it ("PRE"/"POST") and you don't need Live automation storage.
- vs ubutton — a transparent rectangular hit-area (no fill, no text) for overlaying clickable zones on custom art; never user-facing on its own.

RULE OF THUMB: momentary action that stores nothing → textbutton (mode 0). On/off that must be saved/automated → live.text. On/off, no label, no automation needed → live.toggle.
add_textbutton.sizing:
Compact premium device guidance (presentation_rect w,h):
- Standard action button (text like "Reset", "Apply"): ~52–64 x 18 px. Height 18 reads as one dense row aligned with live.numbox/live.menu (also ~18 tall).
- Tight/inline toggle button ("PRE"/"POST", "A"/"B"): ~34–44 x 16 px.
- Prominent primary action ("RENDER", "PANIC"): ~80–110 x 20–22 px.
- Min sensible width ≈ ~0.55 * fontsize * max(len(text), len(texton)) + 2*spacing_x (spacing_x default 4). With fontsize 10 and ~6 chars that's ≈ 41 px; pad to avoid right-truncation (truncate default 1 clips silently). For toggle mode size to the LONGER of text/texton.
- Min sensible height ≈ fontsize + 2*spacing_y + a few px ≈ fontsize+10. fontsize 10 → ~16–18 px.
- fontsize 9–10 for dense rails, 11–12 for a single hero button. Keep spacing_x/spacing_y at 4 (default) or drop to 2 for very tight buttons. rounded 0–3 to match themed siblings (tab/toggle).

Concrete factory changes to def ubutton(...) in src/m4l_builder/ui.py (lines 1095-1109):

1. FIX outlettype — current `["bang", "", "", "int"]` is wrong. Per docs the four outlets are bang/bang/int/list:
   "outlettype": ["bang", "bang", "int", "list"],
   (keep numoutlets=4, numinlets=1.)

2. DO NOT add active*/bgcolor mirroring. ubutton has no active*/bgcolor/bgoncolor/slidercolor attributes — the ACTIVE* COLOR RULE does not apply here (it's a vanilla Max object, not live.*). Adding those keys is silently ignored at best. This is the key divergence from the live.button/live.toggle hardening.

3. Add themeable, correct-by-default keyword args (only the attrs that actually exist):
   def ubutton(id, rect, *, varname=None, patching_rect=None,
               hltcolor=None, rounded=None, hilite=1, toggle=0,
               dragtrack=0, **kwargs):
   - hilite: int (default 1 = flash on click; pass 0 for a silent invisible hot-zone over a graphic that gives its own feedback).
   - hltcolor: 4-float RGBA — set this to the device ACCENT so the click flash is on-theme (the theme injector should target hltcolor, NOT activebgoncolor).
   - rounded: float — match the radius of the panel/graphic it overlays.
   - toggle: int default 0 (button/momentary). Only set 1 for a latching hot-zone.
   - dragtrack: int default 0; set 1 when using outlet 4 to scrub/hit-test.
   Only emit each key when not None (mirror the toggle()/tab() `if x:` pattern, lines 270-275).

4. Set varname when provided (so the box is scriptable / theme-targetable), like other factories.

5. GOTCHA — Z-ORDER: ubutton must sit ON TOP of the graphic it activates. Panels use background:1 to drop behind controls (see ui.py panel + reference doc line 116); ubutton must NOT set background:1 — leave background=0 (default) and add the ubutton AFTER the graphic in the patch so it's above it. If it ends up behind, clicks never reach it.

6. GOTCHA — fully transparent by default: with hilite left default it still flashes; for a truly invisible overlay (e.g. over a v8ui knob that draws its own press state) pass hilite=0. Don't rely on color/bgcolor for fill — neither fills the rect in presentation.

THEME INJECTOR NOTE: the kit's active*-mirroring inject path should SKIP ubutton (maxclass=="ubutton") for the active* keys and instead, if it themes anything, set hltcolor to the accent. Otherwise the active* keys are written and ignored.
add_ubutton.doc_section:
## ✅ add_ubutton  (ubutton)

`ubutton` = a **transparent clickable rectangle** you lay **over** graphics (an image, a
`panel` fill, a v8ui/jsui control, a compiled display, a `swatch`) to make them clickable.
It is a **vanilla Max object, NOT a `live.*` parameter** — so the **active\* color rule does
NOT apply** (it has no `activebgcolor`/`bgoncolor`/`slidercolor`; setting them is ignored).
The only thing it draws is an optional click-flash highlight.

- **`toggle` [int] 0** — `0` = button/momentary (the normal use), `1` = latching toggle.
- **`stay` [atom_long] 0** — "Stay-on Value." `0` = button mode (flash + bang per click).
  Keep `0` for a trigger; non-zero latches.
- **`hilite` [int] 1** — show the click-flash highlight. **Set `0` for a truly invisible
  hot-zone** (when the graphic underneath supplies its own pressed look, e.g. a v8ui button).
- **`hltcolor` [4 floats RGBA]** — the **highlight/flash color = the one attr to theme**
  (point the theme injector here, NOT at any `active*` key). **`rounded` [float]** matches
  the corner radius of the graphic it overlays.
- **`color` [4 floats]** — object-box **outline only** (edit-time); invisible in presentation,
  not a fill. There is **no fill attribute** — transparency is the whole point.
- **`dragtrack` [int] 0** — `1` streams continuous `[x y]` out outlet 4 while dragging
  (cursor hit-testing / scrub strips — something native widgets can't do).
- **`parameter_enable` [int] 0** — OFF by default; ubutton is **not automatable** unless you
  flip it. For a plain trigger, leave it off. (`unitstyle`/`needlemode` do not apply.)

**Outlets (4), documented order — the kit's `outlettype` must be `["bang","bang","int","list"]`:**
1. `bang` on mouse **release** · 2. `bang` on mouse **click/down** · 3. `int` `1`/`0` =
cursor inside/outside on release · 4. `list [x y]` cursor offset from the rect's top-left.

**Sizing:** size the `presentation_rect` to **match the graphic it covers** (not a generic
widget size); match `rounded` to that graphic. Standalone hot-zone ≥16–18 px to stay tappable;
full-panel zone stays ≥4–6 px inside the panel frame so the flash doesn't bleed.

**Z-order gotcha:** ubutton must sit **on top** of its graphic — do **not** set `background 1`
(that's for `panel`); add the ubutton **after** the artwork so it's above it, or clicks never land.

**When to use:** make an already-drawn graphic clickable, or when you need separate press/release
bangs or cursor coordinates. For a normal automatable "looks-like-a-button" trigger use
**`add_button` (live.button)**; for on/off use **`add_toggle`/`add_live_text`** — those are
native-first and themed via the `active*` rule. Reserve a custom v8ui button for bespoke drawn
press/hover artwork (and even then, overlay a `ubutton hilite 0` for the click).
add_ubutton.when_to_use:
vs a custom v8ui control: Use add_ubutton when you've ALREADY drawn the visual (a compiled fill, a v8ui/jsui glass panel, a swatch, an imported picture, a logo) and just need to make a region of it clickable — ubutton is the cheapest possible "hit zone" (native C, no script). Reach for a custom v8ui button only when the BUTTON ITSELF must be the drawn artwork with bespoke press/hover states and animation — but even then the common pattern is v8ui draws, ubutton (hilite 0) overlays for the click. Use the 4th outlet (dragtrack 1) when you need cursor x/y over the graphic (scrub strips, custom XY hit-testing) — that's a thing native live.* widgets can't give you, and it's lighter than a full v8ui mousedrag handler.

vs sibling NATIVE trigger widgets:
- Prefer live.button when you want an automatable/mappable momentary trigger that DRAWS ITS OWN button and stores 0/1 automation (it has activebgcolor/activebgoncolor — theme via the active* rule). live.button is the native-first default for a normal "trigger" button.
- Prefer live.toggle / live.text for an on/off or labeled mode button (native, automatable, themed via active* keys).
- Prefer the vanilla `button` (bpatcher bang) only outside Live UIs.
- Choose ubutton specifically when (a) the look comes from another object you've already drawn and you just want it clickable, or (b) you need press AND release as separate bangs, or (c) you need cursor coordinates. If you just want "a button that looks like a button and automates," ubutton is the WRONG choice — use live.button.
add_ubutton.sizing:
ubutton draws nothing but the optional highlight, so SIZE IT TO THE GRAPHIC IT COVERS — its presentation_rect should match (or sit a hair inside) the artwork/panel/v8ui it activates, not a generic widget size. Guidance for a compact premium device (168-tall ceiling):
- Overlay use: set presentation_rect == the target graphic's rect exactly (e.g. the v8ui knob's 44x44, the panel button's 60x18). Match `rounded` to the graphic's corner radius so the click-flash aligns.
- Standalone invisible click pad / hot-zone: keep a comfortable touch target ~16-18 px min in either dimension (same floor as a native toggle, reference doc line 150) — smaller is hard to hit.
- Full-panel "click anywhere" zone: span the panel's inner rect, staying ≥4-6 px inside any panel border (frame-margin rule, reference doc ~line 158) so the highlight flash doesn't bleed over the frame.
- If hilite=1, remember the flash fills the WHOLE rect — size it to what should visually flash, not larger.

Emit them into the box only when non-default (mirror the existing `if value is not None` pattern) so JSON stays clean, OR always emit for explicitness — match the kit's existing style (it uses conditional emission for value, so do the same for mode/direction when not None).

2) ui_registry.py DEVICE_WIDGET_SPECS["radiogroup"] — add a theme_mapping (it is currently bare `DeviceWidgetSpec(radiogroup)`, so it gets NO color theming). Use the BARE attrs because radiogroup has no active* twins:
       "radiogroup": DeviceWidgetSpec(
           radiogroup,
           theme_mapping={
               "bgoncolor": "accent",      # selected cell = accent (like toggle's activebgoncolor->accent)
               "bgcolor": "tab_bg",        # off cells reuse the tab unselected fill for visual parity
               "bordercolor": "tab_bg",    # quiet divider; or a dedicated theme attr if one exists
           },
       )
   This is the radiogroup analogue of toggle's {"activebgoncolor":"accent"} and tab's bg/on mapping — but with BARE names, since active* attributes do not exist on this object. Theme._inject_theme only fills keys the caller didn't pass, so explicit colors still win.

3) Do NOT add parameter_arg_index — radiogroup is not a Live parameter and cannot be registered/automated. Leave the spec free of parameter machinery (correct as-is).

GOTCHAS / load:
   - value is mode-dependent: in mode 1 (check), value is a bitmask, not an index — document this so callers don't pass an index expecting a single checkbox.
   - The active* color rule is the ONE convention that does NOT apply here. Reviewers expecting activebgoncolor will be wrong — radiogroup ignores any active* attr you set.
   - Because it's not automatable, never wire radiogroup to a Live parameter for "exclusive enum" use — reach for live.tab/live.menu instead (those carry a real parameter).
add_radiogroup.doc_section:
## radiogroup  (classic radio / checkbox group — NOT a Live param)

- **Classic Max object, not a `live.*` control** → it has **no `active*` colors, no parameter, no automation, no `unitstyle`/`needlemode`**. The colors you SEE are the **bare** attrs (there are no active twins): `bgoncolor` (selected/checked cell = accent), `bgcolor` (off cells), `bordercolor`. The "active* color rule" does **not** apply here.
- `mode` [int]: **0 = Radio (exclusive, pick one)**, 1 = Check (multi-toggle checkboxes). `direction` [int]: **0 = vertical** (default), 1 = horizontal.
- `itemcount` [int]: number of cells (kit default 4). `value` [int]: in mode 0 the **selected index**; in mode 1 a **bitmask** of checked cells. One **`int`** outlet.
- `add_radiogroup` themes `bgoncolor→accent`, `bgcolor→tab_bg`, `bordercolor→tab_bg` (analogue of the toggle/tab mapping, but bare names). It registers **no Live parameter** — radiogroup can't be automated.
- **When to use:** last resort among native selectors. Prefer **`add_tab`** (automatable exclusive enum, ≤4-5 opts) or **`add_menu`/`add_umenu`** (dropdown, many opts). Reach for `radiogroup` only for a **vertical stacked list**, a **multi-select checkbox column** (`mode 1` — neither tab nor menu does this), or a deliberate retro Max look. The label is a separate `add_comment`; radiogroup draws no text.
- **Sizing:** ~16-20 px per cell. Vertical: W 18-26, H ≈ itemcount·18. Horizontal: H 18-22, W ≈ itemcount·20 (at which point prefer `live.tab`).
add_radiogroup.when_to_use:
vs a custom v8ui control: A v8ui radio/segmented control is only worth it when you need a fully bespoke look (icons per option, animated selection, non-rectangular cells) or want the selection to drive an automatable Live parameter via a hidden live.tab — radiogroup itself can't be automated. For a plain stacked list of mutually-exclusive options or a checkbox column, the native radiogroup is lighter and theming-correct, so prefer it over custom.

vs sibling NATIVE selectors (the important call): radiogroup is the LAST resort among native selectors for an exclusive choice, because it is NOT a Live parameter (can't be automated, mapped, or saved as a param). Preference order:
  - live.tab — FIRST choice for an exclusive enum with <=4-5 options and horizontal room. It IS a Live parameter (automatable, mappable) and themes via active* colors. Use this for any "pick one mode" that the user should be able to automate.
  - live.menu / live.umenu — for many options (a dropdown), saving space; also a real Live param (live.menu).
  - radiogroup — only when you specifically want a VERTICAL stacked button list, a MULTI-select checkbox group (mode 1 — neither live.tab nor live.menu does multi-select), or a deliberate retro classic-Max aesthetic, AND you don't need Live automation. If you need both multi-select AND automation, use N live.toggles each bound to a parameter instead.
add_radiogroup.sizing:
Compact premium guidance (no dedicated doc table exists yet, derived from the kit's selector/toggle sizing):
- Each cell wants ~16-20 px in the stacking direction to stay clickable; below ~14 px cells crowd.
- Vertical (direction 0, default): width 18-26 px (just the button + a hair of padding; the LABEL is a separate live.comment beside it — radiogroup itself draws no text), height = itemcount * ~18 px (e.g. 4 items ~= 72 px, 3 items ~= 54 px). Pair with comments to the right for option names.
- Horizontal (direction 1): height 18-22 px, width = itemcount * ~18-22 px; at this point you are visually competing with live.tab — prefer live.tab unless you need multi-select.
- Keep itemcount small (<=5-6) for a compact device; longer lists belong in a live.menu dropdown.
- Border: 1 px; let bgoncolor (accent) carry the selection read, bgcolor a muted off fill, so it matches the dense Rupture look.

Concrete changes to def multislider(...) in /Users/squidbot/Projects/m4l-builder/src/m4l_builder/ui.py:633:

1. FIX THE DOCSTRING enum. Replace "setstyle: 0=bar, 1=line, 2=point." with the correct enum: 0=thin line, 1=bars, 2=thin line+peak, 3=point scroll, 4=line scroll, 5=reverse bars. Change the default from setstyle=0 to setstyle=1 (bars) — bars are the premium step/band-readout look; thin-line (0) reads as a faint scribble.

2. DO NOT add active* colors and DO NOT add parameter_enable/unitstyle/ParameterSpec. multislider is not a live.* widget and not an automatable param — the active* color rule explicitly does NOT apply here. This is the one widget in the hardening set where mirroring to active* attrs is WRONG. Theme via the bare attrs only.

3. ADD bgcolor as a first-class kwarg and emit it (mirror the existing `if slidercolor:` pattern). Default it to the device panel/background color so bars render on-theme instead of Max grey:
   def multislider(id, rect, *, size=16, min_val=0.0, max_val=1.0, setminmax=None,
                   slidercolor=None, bgcolor=None, peakcolor=None, candicane2=None,
                   contdata=None, orientation=0, setstyle=1, patching_rect=None, **kwargs)
   ... and after the slidercolor block:
       if bgcolor:   box["bgcolor"]   = bgcolor
       if peakcolor: box["peakcolor"] = peakcolor
       if contdata is not None: box["contdata"] = contdata

4. THEME WIRING: in the theme-inject mapping (the same place tab/dial active* colors are mapped), map multislider -> slidercolor = accent, bgcolor = panel/background, peakcolor = accent2. There is NO active* twin to mirror; this is a deliberate exception to the "mirror to active*" rule documented for the live.* widgets.

5. MODE INTENT: document/param the two uses. DISPLAY mode (default for a readout): contdata irrelevant, you push a value list into the left inlet each frame. EDIT mode: pass contdata=1 so dragged values stream out live; otherwise they only emit on mouse-up.

GOTCHA / LOAD: multislider does NOT persist its displayed values across device reloads the way a param does — if you want the bars to restore, you must re-send the value list on loadbang (or store the list yourself). And because it's not a parameter, it will never appear in Live's "Configure"/automation list — if you need automation per-bar, that's a job for N hidden live.dial params or a custom v8ui, not multislider.
add_multislider.doc_section:
## 🔬 multislider  (`add_multislider`) — multi-bar readout / editor

**Classic Max object, NOT a `live.*` widget.** Two consequences that break the usual rules:

1. **The active\* color rule does NOT apply.** There are no `active*` attributes here.
   Theme with the **bare** attrs — `slidercolor` (bars), `bgcolor` (background),
   `peakcolor` (point markers), `candicane2..candicane8` (multi-color striping). The object
   ignores the Live theme entirely (looks identical bypassed vs active). This is the one kit
   widget where you must **not** mirror to `active*`.
2. **It is not a Live parameter.** No `parameter_enable`, no `unitstyle`, no automation, no
   per-device persistence. Use it as a **display** (push a value list into the left inlet to
   draw N bars) or a lightweight **editor** (user drags bars → list out the left outlet).

**Attributes (CORRECT values):**
- **`size` [int] 16** — number of bars.
- **`setminmax` [lo hi] [0. 1.]** — value range of every bar.
- **`orientation` [int] 0** — `0` = vertical bars left→right (default), `1` = horizontal bars stacked.
- **`setstyle` [int] 1** — **CORRECT enum:** `0` thin line · `1` bars (default, the solid-fill
  step/band look) · `2` thin line + peak · `3` point scroll · `4` line scroll · `5` reverse bars.
  *(The old docstring's "0=bar/1=line/2=point" was wrong.)*
- **`slidercolor` / `bgcolor` / `peakcolor` [4 floats]** — bar fill / background / point markers.
  Set `bgcolor` to the panel color or the bars float on Max grey.
- **`candicane2..8` [4 floats]** — candy-cane bar-color cycling for thin-line styles.
- **`contdata` [int] 0/1** — EDIT mode only: `1` streams values while dragging, `0` only on mouse-up.

**Defaults the factory sets / should set:** `setstyle 1`, emit `bgcolor`/`peakcolor` (theme:
`slidercolor`=accent, `bgcolor`=panel, `peakcolor`=accent2). Do **not** add `parameter_enable`
or `active*` colors.

**When to use:** cheap multi-bar **readout** (spectrum / EQ bands / level bank) or a simple bar
editor. Prefer **`live.grid`/`live.step`** when it's an automatable, device-stored step sequence
(those follow the theme and save); prefer a **custom v8ui** only for drawn curves / breakpoints /
hero visuals; prefer a **row of `live.dial`/`live.slider`** when each value needs its own named
automatable param.

**Sizing (168-tall device):** N vertical bars ≈ `N×6–10 × 24–60` (e.g. 16 bands ≈ 140×40); single
meter `20–30 × 60–120`; keep ≥4–6 px inside the panel frame. Bars <5 px wide → switch to thin-line.

**Gotcha:** values don't persist across reloads (re-send the list on `loadbang`); never appears in
Live's automation list because it isn't a parameter.
add_multislider.when_to_use:
vs a custom v8ui control: use native multislider for a compact, cheap multi-bar READOUT or simple bar editor — spectrum/EQ-band display, a small step-sequencer level row, a histogram, a "16 gains" editor. It draws fast and needs no JS. Reach for a custom v8ui ONLY when you need: a bespoke handle/curve look, draggable breakpoint curves with interpolation, hover/scrub affordances, per-bar labels, or a hero visual — i.e. the same threshold as the rest of the kit (heroes / drag-curves / pads earn v8ui; everything else stays native).

vs sibling native widgets:
- vs live.grid / live.step — those are the LIVE-THEMED step sequencers and ARE automatable Live parameters (store, automate, follow theme). Prefer live.grid/live.step when the thing is an on/off or velocity STEP SEQUENCE that the user expects to save with the device and automate. Use multislider when you want a continuous N-bar display/editor that is NOT a stored parameter (a transient readout, a meter-like bank, a scratch editor) or when you need point/line/scroll styles live.grid can't draw.
- vs nodes — nodes is for free 2D points; multislider is a fixed equally-spaced bank of 1D values.
- vs a row of live.slider / live.dial — use those when each value must be a separate automatable Live parameter with its own name/unit; use multislider when it's one cheap visual bank with no per-value automation.
- vs adsrui — adsrui is a purpose-built envelope; don't fake an envelope with multislider.
add_multislider.sizing:
For a compact premium device (168 px height ceiling):
- A horizontal bank of N vertical bars (orientation 0): width ≈ N × 6–10 px (give each bar ≥6 px so they read; thickness/spacing trim this). Height 24–60 px — ≥24 to register as bars, 40–60 for a satisfying readout. Example 16-band readout: ~120–160 × 40.
- A single tall meter-style multislider: 20–30 × 60–120 (treat like a v-slider; don't exceed ~120 tall).
- A horizontal-orientation stack (orientation 1): each bar is a horizontal lane; total height ≈ N × 5–8 px, width 80–140.
- Keep the rect ≥4–6 px inside the panel border (same frame-margin rule as the rest of the kit) so bgcolor/bars don't get clipped by a panel frame line.
- Bars below ~5 px wide become an unreadable smear — if size is large and width is tight, switch setstyle to 0 (thin line) or 4 (line scroll) instead of 1 (bars).

add_live_grid.correct_defaults:
Concrete changes to def live_grid in src/m4l_builder/ui.py (~L862):
1) ACTIVE* COLOR MIRRORING (the headline fix). Add params: stepcolor, bgstepcolor, bordercolor, marker_horizontalcolor, marker_verticalcolor, bgcolor. For each ON/OFF cell color, set BOTH the bare attr AND its active* twin so one arg themes both states (copy the tab() pattern at L220-235):
   if stepcolor: box["stepcolor"] = stepcolor; box["activestepcolor"] = stepcolor
   if bgstepcolor: box["bgstepcolor"] = bgstepcolor; box["activebgstepcolor"] = bgstepcolor
   if bordercolor: box["bordercolor"] = bordercolor
   if marker_horizontalcolor: box["marker_horizontalcolor"] = marker_horizontalcolor
   if marker_verticalcolor: box["marker_verticalcolor"] = marker_verticalcolor
   if bgcolor: box["bgcolor"] = bgcolor
   Default these to the theme accent (lit cell) and a dim panel grey (empty cell) when the build's theme is applied, exactly like tab/toggle — otherwise cells render default grey ignoring the device theme.
2) MARKER LINES by default: expose marker_horizontal (default 4 when columns%4==0, else None) so a 16-step grid auto-shows 4 beat groups — biggest visual upgrade for near-zero cost.
3) LAYOUT knobs: add spacing (default ~1.0), rounded (default 0), mode (default 0=toggle), matrixmode — only emit when non-default (follow the live_step pattern of `if x is not None`).
4) NO parameter setup: do NOT add unitstyle/needlemode/parameter_enable/saved_attribute_attributes — live.grid is not an automatable scalar param; adding them is wrong and can break the patch. (This is the key gotcha that separates it from dial/tab.)
5) OUTLETS: re-verify numoutlets against the real object (live.step uses 5). Current numoutlets:4 / outlettype ["","","",""] is suspect; align to the live.step-family count once a synced doc confirms, and make outlettype length match.
6) Keep box.update(kwargs) LAST so explicit active* overrides win (already correct).
GOTCHA: marker_horizontal counts cells-per-marker, not number-of-markers (4 = a line every 4 cells). GOTCHA: live.grid needs message-driven init (it does not "load" a default pattern); clearing/seeding the matrix is done by sending it messages at deviceload, not via an attribute.
add_live_grid.doc_section:
## ✅ add_live_grid  (live.grid)

`live.grid` = a `columns × rows` matrix of toggle cells (a step/drum grid) with marker
lines, a playhead/loop overlay, and outlets that report the active column + cell values.
**It is NOT a single automatable Live parameter** — unlike `live.dial`/`live.tab`/`live.toggle`
it has **no** `parameter_enable` / `saved_attribute_attributes` / `varname` / `unitstyle` /
`needlemode`. Its matrix state is patch-stored and driven by **messages**; don't register it
via the parameter path. (Its dedicated Max ref page isn't in the synced cache yet — values
below are from the live.grid spec + our live.* conventions; re-verify outlet count on next
docs sync.)

- **`columns` / `rows` [int]** (default 16 / 8): matrix dimensions (steps × lanes).
- **`mode` [int] 0**: `0 = Toggle` cells (on/off, default) · `1 = value-per-column`
  (a click sets a 0..rows-1 value, multislider-like). Use `0` for a trigger grid.
- **`marker_horizontal` / `marker_vertical` [int]**: draw a heavier divider every **N
  cells** (set `marker_horizontal 4` to split 16 steps into 4 beats — the biggest
  "looks pro" win). `marker_horizontalcolor` / `marker_verticalcolor` [4 floats]: dim accent.
- **`spacing` [float]**: px gap between cells (~1–2). **`rounded` [float]**: cell corner.
- **`direction` [int]** / **`matrixmode` [int]**: read direction / edit mode.
- **Colors follow the active\* rule** (device is active=1 → the colors you SEE are the
  `active*` twins; bare ones only show bypassed): **`activestepcolor`** = lit/ON cell
  (THE accent) / `stepcolor` bypassed · **`activebgstepcolor`** = empty cell / `bgstepcolor`
  bypassed · `bordercolor` = cell + frame · `bgcolor` = panel behind. The kit mirrors each
  bare color to its `active*` twin (one arg themes both states; explicit `active*` via kwargs
  still wins) — same fix as `tab()`/`toggle()`.
- **Sizing**: ~12–16 px/cell. 16×4 trigger row ≈ 208 × 56; 8×8 hero ≈ 130 × 130. Width ≈
  `columns*(cell+spacing)`, height ≈ `rows*(cell+spacing)`; cells ≥10 px to stay clickable,
  ≤18 px so the grid doesn't dominate. Keep ≥4–6 px inside any panel frame.
- **When to use**: the native default for any 2D step/drum matrix. Prefer over `live.step`
  (which is step-rows-of-parameters — pitch+vel+dur per step) when you want a true row×col
  ON/OFF lattice; over `matrixctrl` when you want Live theming + markers + playhead; over a
  custom **v8ui** matrix unless you need per-cell glyphs, velocity-height bars, or animation.
- **Gotchas**: `marker_horizontal` is cells-PER-marker, not marker count. `live.grid` has no
  default pattern — seed/clear it by sending **messages at deviceload**, not via an attribute.
  Do NOT add parameter attrs (`unitstyle`/`needlemode`/`parameter_enable`) — they don't apply
  and can break the box.
add_live_grid.when_to_use: vs custom v8ui: use NATIVE live.grid for any standard step grid / drum-matrix / trigger lattice — it is the premium default (native-first rule), it draws crisp themed cells cheaply and handles toggle/drag/marker/playhead for free. Only reach for a custom v8ui matrix when you need something native genuinely can't draw: per-cell glyphs/icons, velocity-as-height bars with custom shading, curved/iso layouts, animated cell trails, or a piano-roll-style variable-width lattice. vs sibling native widgets: prefer live.grid over (a) live.step when you want a true 2D matrix (pitch x time, or N independent trigger lanes) rather than live.step's step-rows-of-parameters model — live.step is better for "16 steps each carrying pitch+velocity+duration+chance" lanes, live.grid for "which of these row x col cells are ON"; (b) matrixctrl when you want Live theming + markers + a playhead and value-mode cells (matrixctrl is the bare Max router matrix, no live.* theming, no parameter look); (c) multislider when the data is one row of continuous bars, not a toggled 2D grid. If you only need a single on/off, that's a live.toggle, not a grid.
add_live_grid.sizing: Cell-driven, not control-cell-driven. Target ~12-16 px per cell so it reads + is clickable on a compact (≤168 tall) device. A 16x8 grid at 12 px cells + 1 px spacing ≈ 16*13 = 208 W x 8*13 = 104 H — wide but short; a 16x4 trigger grid ≈ 208 x 56 fits a single device row comfortably. Rules of thumb: width ≈ columns*(cell+spacing), height ≈ rows*(cell+spacing); keep cells ≥10 px or they're un-clickable, ≤18 px or the grid dominates. Use marker_horizontal=4 to add visual beat grouping without extra height. Keep the grid ≥4-6 px inside any surrounding panel frame (same frame-margin rule as other widgets, so the bordercolor/panel line isn't clipped). For a hero 8x8 step matrix budget ~130 x 130; for a compact 16-step single-lane trigger row, ~200 x 16-24.

add_live_step.doc_section:
## ✅ add_live_step  (live.step)

`live.step` = the native multi-lane **step sequencer / pattern editor**: `nstep` columns × `nseq` stacked value lanes (e.g. pitch / velocity / gate). It is **NOT a single Live parameter** — no `parameter_enable`, can't be host-mapped; it owns its sequence data, recalled via `pattrstorage`/preset, and is driven by a step bang on its inlet. The 5 outlets emit the active step's per-lane values + index + loop state.

- **`nstep`** [int, 16] — number of steps (columns), 1–16.
- **`nseq`** [int, 1] — stacked value **lanes** per step (1–8); each lane is an independent series (lane0=pitch, lane1=velocity, …). Raising this is the main reason to pick live.step over `multislider`.
- **`mode`** [int, per-lane] — lane display/edit style: `0` slider/bar (default), `1` dial, `2` toggle. Runtime message `mode <seq> <style>`.
- **`range`** / per-lane min–max [list, 0..127] — a lane's value span (e.g. `0 1` for a gate). **No `unitstyle` / `needlemode`** — these are native data values, not a dB/Hz/% param. Don't copy the dial's enums here.
- **`loop_start` / `loop_end`** [int] — the loop bracket. **`marker`** — playhead step.
- **THE active* color rule (device is active=1, so only the active* twins are visible):**
  | You want to color…       | Use (active)            | Bare (bypassed only) |
  |--------------------------|-------------------------|----------------------|
  | lit step value bar       | **`activesteponcolor`** | `steponcolor`        |
  | empty step cell bg       | **`activestepoffcolor`**| `stepoffcolor`       |
  | value fill               | **`activeslidercolor`** | `slidercolor`        |
  | backing panel            | **`activebgcolor`**     | `bgcolor`            |
  | loop bracket / playhead  | `loopcolor` / `markercolor` | — |
  The factory mirrors each color arg to its `active*` twin (one arg themes both states, like `tab()`), and seeds `activesteponcolor` from the theme accent so bars aren't Live grey. Explicit `active*` via `**kwargs` wins.
- **⚠️ Persistence gotcha:** live.step stores its pattern as **sequence data, not a saved attribute** — it will NOT survive a Live preset reload unless bound to a **`pattrstorage`** (give it a `varname`, add to pattr scope) or a `preset` object. Wire that or the user loses their pattern on reopen.
- **When to use:** multi-lane editable PATTERN (16-step pitch+velocity+gate, mod sequencer). Native is the premium default here. vs **`live.grid`** — grid = binary on/off trigger cells; live.step = continuous per-step VALUES. vs **`multislider`** — multislider = one bare lane of bars; live.step = Live-themed stacked lanes + loop region + mixed lane modes. vs **N `live.dial`s** — dials are independent knobs; live.step is a time-ordered, scrub/draw sequence (but is NOT automatable — keep host params off it).
- **Sizing:** ~10 px/step wide (16 steps ≈ 160 px), ~28–34 px per lane tall; a 16×3 hero ≈ 160×96 within the 168 ceiling. Keep step width ≥7 px or steps become un-clickable; lanes ≥22 px to stay editable; ≥4–6 px inside the panel frame.
add_live_step.when_to_use: Use add_live_step when the device needs an editable multi-step PATTERN with several value lanes per step (classic 16-step seq: pitch + velocity + gate, or an arpeggiator/mod sequencer). It is the native premium choice over custom v8ui here — native gives you drag-to-draw, per-lane display modes, loop brackets, and pattr recall for free, none of which is worth re-drawing in v8ui (reserve v8ui only if you need a bespoke non-grid layout or animated playhead overlay native can't draw). vs sibling NATIVE widgets: choose live.step over `live.grid` when each step needs a CONTINUOUS value per lane (velocity 0-127, pitch) rather than grid's on/off cells — live.grid is the binary trigger matrix, live.step is the value sequencer. Choose it over `multislider` when you want Live-themed step lanes + loop region + multiple stacked lanes with mixed display modes (multislider is a single bare lane of bars). Choose it over N separate `live.dial`s when the values are a time-ordered sequence the user scrubs/draws across, not independent knobs. Do NOT use it for a single value (use live.dial/live.numbox) — it cannot be a mapped Live parameter, so anything you want host-automatable must NOT live here.
add_live_step.sizing:
Compact premium guidance for the 168-px-tall device ceiling (per docs sizing tables):
- Per-step column width ≈ 8–12 px; a full 16-step single-lane row at 10 px/step ≈ 160 px wide. Budget width = `nstep * ~10` + a few px frame.
- Height per lane ≈ 22–40 px; a single value lane reads well at ~28–34 px tall. Multi-lane (nseq=2–3, pitch+velocity+gate) stack, so total height ≈ `nseq * ~30` — at nseq=3 you are ~90 px, which fits under 168 with a title + transport row.
- Keep the whole widget ≥4–6 px inside the panel border (frame-clip rule) and give each lane ≥22 px or the drag target gets too thin to edit.
- For a hero 16×3 sequencer pin it ~160×96 px; for an inline 8-step mod lane ~80×28 px (nstep=8, nseq=1). Wider-than-tall is the natural read; don't shrink step width below ~7 px or steps become un-clickable.

add_matrixctrl.correct_defaults:
Concrete changes to def matrixctrl(...) in src/m4l_builder/ui.py (~line 1076):

1) DO NOT mirror colors to active* twins. matrixctrl is a plain Max object, not a live.* param — there is no activebgcolor/activecolor. This is the single biggest gotcha and the inverse of the tab()/dial() pattern. Theme it with exactly three args: bgcolor (background), color (ON cell), elementcolor (OFF cell). For the RUPTURE look: bgcolor ≈ panel/recessed dark, color = accent (ON), elementcolor = a dim background-tinted "off" so the unlit grid still reads.

2) Add a range parameter (default 2) and emit it. Most uses are binary; expose it so multi-state grids work. Cell values are then 0..range-1.

3) Add spacing/margin args and emit them so the grid is legible: signature gain horizontalspacing/verticalspacing (default ~1) and horizontalmargin/verticalmargin (default 1). A spacing of 0 makes cells touch and read as one block — 1–2px reads as a grid.

4) Add the exclusivity option as a clean kwarg, e.g. exclusive: str = None mapping to one/matrix|one/row|one/column. Because the attribute key contains a slash, set it via box["one/row"] = 1 etc. (the **kwargs path can't express a slash as a Python identifier, so this needs explicit handling in the factory).

5) Add dialmode passthrough (default 0) and clickvalue if you want momentary/fixed-value clicks; leave active at the implicit default 1.

6) unitstyle / needlemode / parameter setup: NOT APPLICABLE. matrixctrl is not an automatable Live parameter and has no unitstyle, no needlemode, no parameter_enable/saved_attribute_attributes. Do NOT add the ParameterSpec/_resolve_parameter_spec plumbing that dial()/tab() use. If you ever need automation you'd drive it from a sibling live.* control, not matrixctrl itself.

7) Keep the existing presentation/patching_rect plumbing (it's correct). numoutlets should be 2 with outlettype ["", ""] (left list out, right getrow/getcolumn out) — current code already has this.

8) GOTCHA / load behavior: matrixctrl does NOT persist its cell states across save/load by default — it loads cleared. If the device needs a default pattern, send the cells via a loadbang → message/pattr after instantiation; the box attributes only configure geometry/colors, not contents. Also: with no spacing and a small rect, cells can collapse below ~6px and become unclickable — size for ≥8px cells (see sizing).
add_matrixctrl.doc_section:
## ✅ add_matrixctrl  (matrixctrl)

`matrixctrl` = a grid of clickable cells (a button matrix), `rows` × `columns`, each cell
holding an integer state `0..range-1`. **It is a plain Max UI object, NOT a `live.*`
parameter** — so it is **not automatable**, takes no `parameter_enable`/`unitstyle`/
`needlemode`, and — the key gotcha — **the active\* color rule does NOT apply.** Theme it
with just three colors; there are no `active*` twins.

- **`rows` [atom_long] 4** · **`columns` [atom_long] 8** — grid size (our kit defaults to 8×8).
- **`range` [atom_long] 2** — states per cell. `2` = binary on/off; `>2` = multi-state
  (cell value `0..range-1`). (Old docs called this `states`; current name is `range`.)
- **Colors (the ONLY theming hooks — no `active*` variants):**
  - **`bgcolor` [4 floats]** — matrix background.
  - **`color` [4 floats]** — a cell in the **ON** state (use the accent).
  - **`elementcolor` [4 floats]** — a cell in the **OFF** state (use a dim bg tint so the
    unlit grid still reads).
- **No `cellsize` attribute** — cell px is computed:
  `cellW = (rectW − 2·horizontalmargin − (cols−1)·horizontalspacing) / columns` (same for H).
  Size the bounding rect; cells fall out of it. Keep cells **≥ 8px** or they go unclickable.
  - **`horizontalspacing` / `verticalspacing` [atom_long] 0** — px gap between cells (use **1–2**;
    `0` makes the grid read as a solid block).
  - **`horizontalmargin` / `verticalmargin` [atom_long] 1** — px inset from the bounding edge.
  - **`scale` [int] 1** — scale graphics on resize. **`autosize` [int] 0**.
- **Exclusivity (radio behavior) — attribute keys contain a slash:**
  **`one/matrix`** (≤1 non-zero cell total), **`one/row`** (≤1 per row), **`one/column`**
  (≤1 per column), all `[int] 0`. Set via `box["one/row"] = 1` (can't go through `**kwargs`).
- **Interaction:** **`dialmode` [int] 0** (0 click · 1 indexed click-drag · 2 gain float
  click-drag) · **`dialtracking` [int] 0** · **`clickvalue` [atom_long] −1** (≥0 ⇒ a click
  sets that fixed value) · **`active` [int] 1** (0 = display-only, ignores clicks).
- **I/O:** `numinlets 1`, `numoutlets 2`, `outlettype ["", ""]` — left outlet emits the
  list `col row value` on change; right outlet returns `getrow`/`getcolumn` queries.
- **Set/read a cell:** list **`col row value`** (0-indexed, **top-left origin**). **`clear`**
  zeroes the whole grid.

**Gotcha — contents don't persist:** matrixctrl loads **cleared**; box attributes set only
geometry/colors, never cell contents. For a default pattern, push cells from a
`loadbang` → message/`pattr` after instantiation.

**Kit defaults (factory):** mirror `bgcolor`/`color`/`elementcolor` straight through (NO
`active*` mirroring — that's the live.tab/dial pattern and is wrong here); expose `range`
(default 2), spacing/margin (default 1), and an `exclusive=` kwarg → `one/row|column|matrix`.
**Do NOT** add `ParameterSpec`/`unitstyle`/`needlemode` — matrixctrl is not a Live param.

**When to use:** a literal 2D grid of on/off (or multi-state) toggles where the layout IS
the meaning — step-sequencer pad grid, trigger/euclidean banks, patch/routing matrix.
Prefer **custom v8ui** only for per-cell color/gradient, animated playheads, drag-to-paint
curves, or non-rectangular geometry. Prefer **`multislider`/`live.grid`/`live.step`** when
each column is a continuous value or you need Live automation (those ARE `live.*` params,
themed via `active*`); prefer **`live.tab`/`radiogroup`** for a single 1-of-N row.

**Sizing:** cell ≥ 8px clickable, 10–14px comfortable, 16–18px touch pad. 4×4 pad ≈ 76×76px;
a 16-step row at 12px ≈ 209px wide / ~14px tall — an 8×16 grid (~209×116) nears the 168
device-height ceiling, so cap at ≤8 rows or paginate. `spacing 1–2` + `margin 1` is the
compact sweet spot.
add_matrixctrl.when_to_use:
Use add_matrixctrl for a literal grid of on/off (or multi-state) toggles where the 2D layout IS the meaning: a step sequencer's pitch×step pad grid, a euclidean/trigger pattern bank, a routing/patch matrix (input row → output column), a per-step velocity or mute grid, a mod-routing matrix. Reach for it when the user needs to click many cells fast and see the whole pattern at a glance.

vs a custom v8ui control: matrixctrl is the native-first answer for a plain rectangular grid of equal cells — it draws cheaply in compiled code, supports range>2 multi-state cells, and the one/row|column|matrix radio-exclusivity for free. Build custom v8ui ONLY when you need per-cell colors/gradients, non-rectangular geometry, drag-to-paint curves across cells, animated playhead overlays, or a velocity ramp drawn inside each cell — things matrixctrl cannot render. For a sequencer that needs a moving playhead column highlight, matrixctrl alone can't animate it; pair it with an overlay or go custom.

vs sibling native widgets: use live.tab/live.menu/radiogroup for a 1-of-N single-row choice (not a 2D grid). Use multislider / live.grid / live.step when each column is a continuous VALUE (bar height, velocity ramp) rather than a discrete state, or when you specifically want Live's sequencer look + automation — live.grid/live.step ARE live.* params (automatable, themed via active*), matrixctrl is not. Use a single live.toggle for one boolean. Choose matrixctrl when you want a dense uniform button grid and don't need per-cell Live automation.
add_matrixctrl.sizing:
No cellsize attr — cell px = (rect_w − 2·horizontalmargin − (cols−1)·horizontalspacing) / columns, same vertically. So you size the BOUNDING rect and let cells fall out.

Premium compact targets:
- Cell ≥ 8px clickable; 10–14px reads as a comfortable pad; ≥16px for a finger/touch-style trigger pad.
- 16-step × N-pitch sequencer row: with margin 1 + spacing 1, a 16-col row at 12px cells ≈ 16·12 + 15·1 + 2 = 209px wide; one pitch row ≈ 14px tall. An 8×16 grid ≈ 209 × ~116px — at the edge of the 168 device-height ceiling, so prefer ≤8 rows or split into pages.
- Trigger/pad bank 4×4: 16–18px cells → ~76 × 76px square block, sits nicely beside knobs.
- Patch/routing matrix: keep cells 10–12px so labels can flank the grid; reserve ~12px gutters for row/column captions (comments).
- Spacing 1–2px + margin 1px is the sweet spot — spacing 0 makes the grid read as a solid block; >3px wastes the compact budget. Keep the whole grid ≥4–6px inside any enclosing panel frame (same frame-margin rule as the rest of the kit).

   "numinlets": 10,
   "numoutlets": 10,
   "outlettype": ["", "", "", "", "", "", "", "", "", ""],
   (Keeping 1/4 risks mis-wiring patchlines and breaks compatibility with the live.adsr~ pairing the maxref documents.)

Add kwargs linecolor, handlecolor, slopehandlecolor (plus keep bgcolor/bordercolor/focusbordercolor) and for each, set BOTH the bare attr and its active* twin so one arg themes active(=what you see) and bypassed:
   if linecolor:        box["linecolor"] = linecolor;        box["activelinecolor"] = linecolor
   if handlecolor:      box["handlecolor"] = handlecolor;    box["activehandlecolor"] = handlecolor
   if slopehandlecolor: box["slopehandlecolor"] = slopehandlecolor; box["activeslopehandlecolor"] = slopehandlecolor
   Also accept explicit activelinecolor=/activehandlecolor=/activeslopehandlecolor= overrides that win if passed.

3) ADD THE TWO M4L SAFETY DEFAULTS (load gotcha). Because this device persists ADSR values and is usually wired to value-storing objects, default to the conflict-free mode the maxref prescribes:

   tethering:  int = 0   ->  box["tethering"]  = tethering       # don't echo incoming values during automation
   (Default both to 0 for the M4L case; expose as kwargs so a standalone/Max use can set 1.)

4) NO needlemode / NO unitstyle / NO parameter setup. adsrui is not a live.dial and not a Live parameter — do NOT call _resolve_parameter_spec, do NOT set parameter_enable, do NOT add unitstyle or needlemode (those concepts don't exist on this object). Leave it parameter-less like scope(). Shape/time defaults (attack_time/decay_time/sustain/release_time/*_domain) should stay caller-provided kwargs; only pass through when given.

5) OPTIONAL editor-feel kwargs to expose (pass-through only when set): show_slopehandles (0 default like Live), enable_peak/enable_initial/enable_final, attack_domain/decay_domain/release_domain to cap the drag ranges. Keep box.update(kwargs) last so callers can still set anything.
add_adsrui.doc_section:
## ✅ add_adsrui  (live.adsr~ / adsrui)

`live.adsrui` = the graphical **ADSR envelope EDITOR** — the user drags Attack / Decay /
Sustain / Release handles, each segment with its own length + exponential slope. It's the
UI companion to `live.adsr~` (the sample-accurate signal generator, 4 args:
`attack decay sustain release`). This is the one "drag-curve" control native Max gives us
for free — **use it instead of a custom v8ui ADSR** unless you need a look the panel/material
kit can't skin. Ground truth lives in the install (no doc-cache HTML exists):
`…/maxforlive-elements/docs/live.adsrui.maxref.xml` + the `.maxhelp` boxes.

**Not a Live parameter.** Unlike `live.dial`, adsrui has **no `parameter_enable`, no
`unitstyle`, no `needlemode`** — it's a styled editor like `live.scope~`. Its values persist
via `outputmode`, and to make automatable A/D/S/R you pair it with `live.numbox`/`live.dial`
that own the real params.

**Geometry (factory MUST match Live):** real boxes are **`numinlets` 10 / `numoutlets` 10**
with **10 empty-string `outlettype`s** — not 1/4. (The old factory's 1/4 mis-wires.)

**Shape attrs** (you set these as kwargs; pass-through only when given):
- `attack_time` / `decay_time` / `release_time` [float ms] — clamped to the matching
  `*_domain`. `sustain` [0..1] level held after decay. `peak` / `initial` / `final` [0..1].
- `attack_slope` / `decay_slope` / `release_slope` [-1..1] — `0` linear, ± = curves.
- `attack_domain` / `decay_domain` / `release_domain` [`[min,max]` ms] — **caps handle
  travel**; set the domain before/with the time or the handle won't reach.
- `enable_peak` (1 = peak moves H+V, 0 = H only) · `enable_initial` · `enable_final` ·
  `show_slopehandles` (Live default **0**) · `show_bounds`.

**Color rule (the usual active\* trap).** A device runs `active = 1`, so the colors you SEE
are the `active*` twins; the bare ones only show when bypassed. The factory **mirrors** each:

| You want to color…        | Use (active state)         | Bare (bypassed only)  |
|---------------------------|----------------------------|-----------------------|
| envelope curve            | **`activelinecolor`**      | `linecolor`           |
| A/D/S/R handles           | **`activehandlecolor`**    | `handlecolor`         |
| slope handles             | **`activeslopehandlecolor`** | `slopehandlecolor`  |
| background / frame        | `bgcolor` / `bordercolor` / `focusbordercolor` |       |

`add_adsrui(linecolor=…, handlecolor=…, slopehandlecolor=…)` sets **both** the bare attr and
its `active*` twin (explicit `active*` args win).

**M4L load/automation gotchas (factory defaults `0`):**
- **`outputmode` 0** — default ON re-emits stored values on load and fights value-storing
  objects (`live.numbox`). The maxref tells you to turn it **off** in M4L. (If nothing else
  seeds initial values, send them yourself from a loadbang.)
- **`tethering` 0** — default ON echoes incoming values straight back out → glitchy
  automation / stack overflow on feedback wiring. Disable in M4L.

**Sizing:** compact ~120–160 × 56–72 · standard hero ~**198 × 95** (Live's own) ·
large ~280–450 × 150–207. Keep H ≥ ~56 so handles stay grabbable; draw a label `comment`
above (the object shows no name).

**When to use:** native adsrui for any standard A/D/S/R where the **shape/gesture** matters;
four `live.dial`s (or `live.numbox`) when you only need the scalars as automatable params;
a custom jsui/v8ui ADSR only for >4 stages (DAHDSR) or a curve look the kit can't draw. Pair
with `live.adsr~` when you also need the audio-rate envelope signal.
add_adsrui.when_to_use:
vs a custom v8ui control: adsrui IS the bespoke draggable-curve control native Max gives you for free — it's exactly the "drag-curve / multi-handle editor" case the kit normally reserves v8ui for. Use native adsrui as the hero envelope editor; only drop to a custom jsui/v8ui ADSR if you need a look the kit's panel/material system can't skin (custom segment shading, labels baked into the curve, non-ADSR shapes, or >4 stages / DAHDSR). For a standard A/D/S/R, native wins on accuracy, keyboard nudge, and zero CPU.

vs sibling native widgets: if you only need the four scalar numbers (no drawn curve), four compact live.dial knobs (add_dial, unitstyle 2 = time for A/D/R, percent or float for S) or live.numbox are denser and each becomes a real automatable Live parameter — adsrui's outputs are NOT parameters by themselves, so for automatable A/D/S/R you typically pair adsrui (the editor) with live.numbox/live.dial that own the params, with outputmode 0 + tethering 0 to avoid the load conflict. Reach for adsrui specifically when the SHAPE (slopes, relative segment lengths, the visual gesture) is the point. Pair the editor with live.adsr~ when you also need the actual sample-accurate signal envelope in the audio path.
add_adsrui.sizing:
Real Live boxes run ~198x95 px (compact) up to ~450x207 px (large). For a compact premium device:
- Compact editor: ~120–160 W x 56–72 H — enough to read the curve and grab handles; show_slopehandles 0 to reduce clutter at this size.
- Standard hero envelope: ~180–220 W x 80–96 H (matches Live's own ~198x95) — the sweet spot; slope handles optional.
- Large/feature: ~280–450 W x 150–207 H when the envelope is the centerpiece and you want slope handles + bounds visible.
Keep H ≥ ~56 px so handles stay grabbable; below that the drag targets get fiddly. Give it a label comment above (the object draws no name). On a 168-tall device, a ~180x80 editor leaves room for a label row above and a value/numbox row below.

add_nodes.correct_defaults:
CONCRETE changes to `def nodes(...)` in src/m4l_builder/ui.py:1049 (current code is at lines 1049–1073):

1) FIX node count attr (highest priority): the param is `nodenumber`, not `numnodes`.
   - Change the signature default arg to drive `box["nodenumber"] = nodenumber` (keep the public kwarg name `nodenumber=4`; if you must keep back-compat, accept `numnodes` as a deprecated alias but WRITE `nodenumber`). Default 4 is fine for a morph pad.

2) DELETE the xmin/xmax/ymin/ymax kwargs and their `if … is not None` blocks. These attributes do not exist on `nodes`; the field is always normalized 0.–1. Replace them with optional layout kwargs that DO exist:
   - `xplace: list = None`, `yplace: list = None`, `nsize: list = None` → emit only when provided, each a list of floats in 0.–1. (len == nodenumber). This lets a builder lay out the N node centers (e.g. evenly around a circle) instead of all stacking at the default.

3) Fix outlettype precision: set `"outlettype": ["list", "list", "list"]` (currently `["", "", ""]`). Keep numoutlets 3, numinlets 1.

4) ADD theming via dynamiccolor mirrors — but note there are NO active* attrs here (it is not a live.* object), so the active*-mirror rule does NOT apply. Instead accept and pass through the real color attrs and let them default to the theme:
   - `bgcolor`, `nodecolor` (the node fill — the most important accent; this is the dynamiccolor `elementcolor`), `pointcolor` (slider center = `accentcolor`), `knobcolor` (slider ring = `selectioncolor`), `textcolor`.
   - For a compact premium device pass the theme's panel bg to `bgcolor`, the accent to `pointcolor`, and a dim accent (~0.4 alpha) to `nodecolor`. Do not set bordercolor/knobbordercolor (obsolete/invisible).

5) Slider visibility default: set `displayknob` explicitly. For a morph-pad you usually WANT the draggable point visible → default `displayknob=1` (Circle) + `knobsize` ~8–10. If you only want to programmatically set the slider via a `list`, keep displayknob=0. Make it a kwarg with default 1.

6) Preset/persistence: nodes is not a Live automation param, so DON'T add a ParameterSpec / saved_attribute_attributes block like the live.* factories do. To recall the node layout, set `parameter_enable=1` (so initial node positions are stored with the device) — or document wiring nodes' left outlet to a `preset`/`pattrstorage`. Add `parameter_enable: 1` to the box for state persistence; leave `parameter_mappable` at its default 1.

7) GOTCHA / load-order: `dynamic` defaults to 1 (Max 9). If the device ever connects nodes' left outlet to a `preset` object, `dynamic` will OVERRIDE your `nodenumber`/`xplace` with the preset count.
